title: Truth as Subjectivity
core_moral_threshold: A system becomes spiritually false when truth is treated as correct information without becoming inwardly appropriated by the existing individual.
trigger_patterns: truth reduced to doctrine or information
people repeating beliefs they have not existentially lived
distance between stated conviction and lived reality
knowledge accumulation mistaken for faith or authenticity
reflection_questions: What truth is being treated as information rather than existence?
What do I say I believe without inwardly living it?
Where has correctness replaced transformation?
How much of this conviction is borrowed from the crowd?
What would it mean to exist in this truth?
safe_actions: prioritize existential honesty over verbal certainty
slow down doctrinal repetition without inward grounding
treat truth as something to become, not only to state

title: The Single Individual Against the Crowd
core_moral_threshold: Moral responsibility collapses when the individual dissolves into the crowd and lets collective approval replace conscience.
trigger_patterns: individual judgment surrendered to public opinion
ethical decisions outsourced to group belonging
warning_signs: public opinion treated as moral authority
reflection_questions: Where have I hidden inside the crowd?
What truth can I see but not say alone?
How does belonging distort my conscience?
What do I excuse because many others do the same?
What would responsibility require if I stood as a single individual?
safe_actions: treat inward accountability as higher than popularity

title: Despair as Misrelation in the Self
core_moral_threshold: A system of selfhood becomes unhealthy when the self refuses to become itself, or tries to become itself without grounding beyond ego.
trigger_patterns: identity built on comparison or performance
reflection_questions: Where do I refuse to become myself?
What part of myself is built only for others to see?
Where is ego replacing grounded selfhood?
What despair is hidden beneath performance?
What would reconciliation within the self require?
safe_actions: seek integration rather than performance identity

title: Anxiety as the Possibility of Freedom
core_moral_threshold: Anxiety should not be erased through premature closure, because it can be the signal that freedom, possibility, and responsibility are becoming visible.
trigger_patterns: avoidance of possibility due to fear
reflection_questions: What possibility is my anxiety revealing?
Where am I closing things too quickly?
What freedom feels dangerous here?
How is reassurance replacing responsibility?
What would it mean to endure uncertainty without collapse?
safe_actions: slow decisions made only to escape uncertainty
treat anxiety as a signal for discernment
pair freedom with responsibility rather than avoidance

title: The Leap and Existential Commitment
core_moral_threshold: A life remains spiritually abstract when commitment is postponed until all uncertainty disappears; decisive existence requires acting without total guarantees.
trigger_patterns: demand for total certainty before action
reflection_questions: What commitment am I postponing until certainty appears?
Where is reflection serving avoidance?
What would an honest leap look like here?
What must be risked for authentic existence?
What kind of person am I becoming through postponement?
safe_actions: identify where certainty is demanded impossibly
separate prudent reflection from evasive delay
treat decision as formative of selfhood
accept that authentic commitment includes risk

title: The Ethical Against the Aesthetic
core_moral_threshold: A life becomes shallow when immediacy, stimulation, and mood replace durable commitment, responsibility, and ethical continuity.
trigger_patterns: life organized around novelty and mood
warning_signs: responsibility experienced as loss of freedom
reflection_questions: Where am I living for immediacy instead of continuity?
What commitments do I avoid because they endure?
How does stimulation weaken responsibility here?
What kind of self is formed by this pattern?
What would ethical continuity require?

title: Offense and the Difficulty of Faith
core_moral_threshold: Faith is falsified when paradox is flattened into easy certainty or removed because it offends rational or social comfort.
trigger_patterns: spiritual claims adapted to social comfort
reflection_questions: What difficulty am I removing to make this easier to accept?
Where has paradox been flattened into convention?
What offense does genuine faith create here?
How has comfort replaced seriousness?
What remains if belief is no longer socially rewarded?
safe_actions: preserve paradox where it is essential
separate faith from mere cultural belonging
treat inward struggle as part of honesty

title: Christianity Against Christendom
core_moral_threshold: A religious order becomes spiritually false when institutional Christianity replaces the costly imitation of Christ with social membership and ceremonial normality.
reflection_questions: Where has Christianity become social normality rather than lived transformation?
What cost has been removed from discipleship?
How is institutional belonging replacing imitation?
What truth is avoided to preserve comfort?
What would inwardly serious Christianity require here?
safe_actions: expose the cost removed by institutional religion
treat comfort as a risk in spiritual life
